concerns.text: Task T009.1 instructs the implementer to 'Update spec.md FR-001' at runtime. This violates the 'Single Source of Truth' principle (Constitution IV) and the project workflow where spec.md is a read-only input for the implementation phase. An implementer cannot execute a task that requires modifying the specification document itself; this requires a formal PR process, not a code task.

Task T023.1 instructs the implementer to 'Update spec.md US-3 Acceptance Scenario 3' to mandate 'Wilcoxon signed-rank test'. Similar to T009.1, this requires modifying the specification document at runtime, which is not an executable code task. The spec amendment should be reflected in the plan or tasks, not executed as a file edit by the implementer.

Task T025 defines 'Identity Mapping Error' as 'predicting the mean of the training labels'. This contradicts the standard definition of Identity Mapping (y_pred = x) and conflicts with the plan's explicit interpretation of FR-007 as 'Mean Predictor Error'. The task description is ambiguous and risks implementing the wrong baseline logic.

Task T023 mandates 'Benjamini-Hochberg correction', while the plan (T023) explicitly requires 'Bonferroni correction' (α = 0.0167). This is a direct contradiction in statistical methodology. The implementer cannot determine which correction to apply without external clarification, making the task non-deterministic.

Task T024 defines the 'Failure Boundary' threshold as 'p < 0.05'. However, the plan (T023) and the amended spec requirement (T023.1) establish a Bonferroni-corrected threshold of 'p < 0.0167'. The task description uses the uncorrected alpha, which would lead to a statistically invalid conclusion if the code follows the text.

Tasks T015 and T016 specify saving model artifacts to 'data/results/', while the plan (T015/T016) and project structure explicitly define the path as 'artifacts/models/'. This path inconsistency breaks the 'Single Source of Truth' and will cause downstream tasks (T021, T022) to fail when attempting to load models from the wrong location.

Task T009 conflates three distinct logical steps (Download, Intersection Filter, and Train/Test Split) into a single task. While the downstream atomizer will split this, the current granularity is too coarse for deterministic execution, as it mixes data acquisition, validation, and splitting logic which have different failure modes and dependencies.

Task T017 calculates the stability ratio and saves 'stability_report.json'. Task T026 redundantly recalculates the same metric and generates a *second* report file. This indicates poor task decomposition and creates ambiguity on which report is the source of truth, risking data inconsistency.
